clinical_criteria: Infant assessment for signs of withdrawal begins before 12 hours of life (first documented summary NOWS score documented between birth date/time and birth date/time +12 hours)
Infant was observed for at least 24 hours after weaning off medications prior to discharge
Denominator exclusions: maternal HIV diagnosis or present/positive/non-zero maternal HIV toxicology OR present/positive/non-zero maternal illicit substance toxicology
Primary medication used to treat infant opioid withdrawal was an opioid (e.g., morphine, methadone)
Duration of pharmacotherapy measured in days
Duration of birth hospitalization measured in days
algorithm_summary: This phenotype defines quality indicators for hospital-based care of opioid-exposed newborns using electronic health record data. It builds upon a predefined population of opioid-exposed infants and measures 8 quality indicators including: NOWS scoring timing, feeding practices (breastfeeding and lactation consultation), infant pharmacotherapy patterns, and length of stay. The algorithm uses both structured and unstructured data elements from Epic EHR during the birth hospitalization and perinatal period. Key variables also include maternal factors like insurance type, smoking status, mental health diagnoses, opioid prescriptions, illicit substance toxicology, and address changes, as well as infant mortality.
